# HQ

> HQ is an AI operations platform — agents, CRM, tasks, knowledge, and routines. Available as a hosted service at app.yourhq.ai ($30/mo per workspace) or self-hosted for free on your own infrastructure. Both run the same open-source codebase. Supabase is the backend; Docker gateway hosts (or cloud sandboxes for hosted) run agents. Each agent can run a different model and thinking level, with per-task overrides for one-off reasoning depth changes.

## Primary docs

- [HQ documentation](https://docs.yourhq.ai): Human-readable documentation home.
- [Why HQ](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/why-hq): Design philosophy and honest comparison against CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, and n8n. Read this if you're deciding whether HQ is the right fit.
- [Choose your path](https://docs.yourhq.ai/getting-started/choose-your-path): Audience-based starting points — hosted, self-host, agent builders, developers, and security reviewers.
- [What HQ includes](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/features): Product tour of agents, per-agent model selection, secrets management, source connectors, CRM, tasks, documents, automations, gateways, usage, and budgets.
- [Roadmap](https://docs.yourhq.ai/getting-started/roadmap): Shipped areas and planned work.

## Hosted

- [Getting started (hosted)](https://docs.yourhq.ai/hosted/getting-started): Sign up, provision a workspace, create your first agent — all from the browser.
- [Account & billing](https://docs.yourhq.ai/hosted/account-billing): Manage workspaces, payment method, cancellation, and the 30-day grace period.
- [Hosted FAQ](https://docs.yourhq.ai/hosted/faq): Pricing, data ownership, cancellation, migration, and differences from self-hosting.

## Self-hosting

- [Quickstart](https://docs.yourhq.ai/getting-started/quickstart): Fastest path from zero to a self-hosted HQ workspace in 10 minutes.
- [Requirements](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/requirements): Infrastructure and accounts needed to run HQ.
- [Supabase setup](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/supabase): Create the Supabase project, run migrations, and configure auth.
- [Installation](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/installation): One-line install, manual install, and development mode.
- [Networking](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/networking): Local, Tailscale, public, and multi-machine deployment topologies.
- [Add a gateway](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/add-gateway): Register another Docker host so it can run agents.
- [Gateway boot sequence](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/gateway-boot): The 13-step entrypoint: credential resolution, git seeding, VNC, secrets sourcing, Supabase registration.
- [Operations](https://docs.yourhq.ai/self-host/operations): Updates, backups, image pinning, and uninstalling.

## Using HQ

- [Workspace setup](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/workspace-setup): Configure workspace profile, pipeline, fields, and streams.
- [Create an agent](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/create-agent): Provision an agent from a template and pair it with Telegram.
- [Connect a model provider](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/connect-model-provider): Add API-key and interactive provider auth. Per-agent model selection, thinking levels, per-task overrides, and multi-route provider handling.
- [Agent boot documents](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/agent-boot-docs): Use document tags to inject shared context into agent sessions.
- [Automations](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/automations): Turn CRM and task events into agent inbox work.
- [Secrets management](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/secrets-management): Store API keys, channel tokens, and OAuth credentials securely. Encrypted at rest, synced to gateways, injected as environment variables.
- [Usage budgets](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/usage-budgets): Track model usage and enforce monthly agent budgets. Model selection cost optimization strategies.
- [Plugins](https://docs.yourhq.ai/guides/plugins): Create, manage, and contribute plugins that extend HQ with custom integrations and automations.

## Concepts

- [Architecture](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/architecture): How the UI, Supabase, gateways, queues, files API, and agents fit together.
- [Agents](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/agents): Agent workspaces, templates, memory, model/thinking configuration, Telegram, tools, org chart, and budgets.
- [Workspaces](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/workspaces): How one UI manages multiple Supabase-backed workspaces.
- [Gateways](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/gateways): Docker hosts that run agents, desktops, files API, and daemons.
- [Knowledge system](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/knowledge): Unified knowledge store (pages, skills, files, sources), source connector plugin architecture, embedding pipeline, and agent boot context.
- [Tasks, inbox, and commands](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/tasks-inbox-and-commands): The queues that coordinate UI, gateways, and agents.
- [Plugins](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/plugins): Event-driven plugin system — local Python handlers and remote webhooks for extending HQ with custom integrations.
- [Security model](https://docs.yourhq.ai/concepts/security-model): Trust boundaries behind HQ's self-hosted architecture.

## Design

- [Design principles](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/overview): The six principles behind HQ's interface — keyboard-first, direct manipulation, dense but calm, one token one truth, multi-view by default, surface hierarchy through opacity.
- [Foundations](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/foundations): Token reference — color (OKLch palette, surface ladder, semantic text, status, priority, charts, sidebar), typography (Geist, six-step scale), radius, spacing, motion, theming, iconography.
- [Primitives](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/components/primitives): Catalog of the 60 building blocks under `apps/ui/src/components/ui/` — shadcn/ui (New York) plus seven HQ extensions, grouped by purpose.
- [Composites](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/components/composites): The 24 reusable compositions under `apps/ui/src/components/shared/` — DataTable, SidePanel, CommandPalette, FolderTree, EmptyState, and more.
- [Layout](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/patterns/layout): Dashboard shell, sidebar widths and groups, header bar ownership, responsive rules, recipe for a new route.
- [Data display](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/patterns/data-display): Multi-view orchestrator pattern (one hook, many views), table/cards/kanban/calendar shapes, detail surfaces (full page vs slide-over vs modal), realtime as default.
- [Creation and editing](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/patterns/creation-and-editing): SidePanel-vs-Dialog rule based on user mode, inline editing patterns, Novel-vs-Textarea decision rule, RHF + Zod forms.
- [Interaction](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/patterns/interaction): Command palette anatomy, full G-then-letter shortcut table, `@dnd-kit` as canonical drag-and-drop, realtime subscription discipline.
- [Feedback](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/patterns/feedback): Toast/dialog/error/empty/skeleton/alert decision tree, golden-path save and delete flows.
- [Contributing](https://docs.yourhq.ai/design/contributing): Where new code goes (ui/ vs shared/ vs feature module), naming conventions, three-question test for promoting components, recipe for a new feature module.

## Reference

- [API reference](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/api): UI HTTP endpoints, Supabase RPCs, key table schemas, enum reference, and common query patterns.
- [Configuration](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/configuration): Environment variables and runtime configuration.
- [Database schema](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/database-schema): Tables, migrations, queues, triggers, and RLS trust model.
- [Migrations](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/migrations): Ordered Supabase migration files and how to apply them.
- [Agent templates](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/templates): Template files, structure, and provisioning behavior.
- [Docker services](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/docker-services): Containers in the HQ Compose stack.
- [Files API](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/files-api): How the UI reads and edits files in agent worktrees. Also serves source connector endpoints (`/sources/validate`, `/sources/browse`).
- [Command lifecycle](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/command-lifecycle): How gateway commands move from UI action to runner execution.
- [Command actions](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/command-actions): All shell, auth, and source actions the runner can execute, with payload schemas. Includes `source_write` for write-back to external sources.
- [Inbox dispatch](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/inbox-dispatch): How the dispatcher wakes agents from inbox items, wake gating conditions, and reconciliation.
- [Plugin events](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/plugin-events): Complete reference of all 16 events available to HQ plugins, with payload schemas and emission sources.
- [Heartbeat](https://docs.yourhq.ai/reference/heartbeat): Gateway liveness signals — Supabase heartbeat, local file, freshness thresholds.

## Development and contribution

- [Local development](https://docs.yourhq.ai/development/local-development): Development setup, project structure, and common tasks.
- [Repo structure](https://docs.yourhq.ai/development/repo-structure): Where major parts of the codebase live.
- [Contributing](https://docs.yourhq.ai/development/contributing): Contribution flow for code, templates, docs, and bug reports.
- [Testing](https://docs.yourhq.ai/development/testing): Automated test suite (Vitest, pytest, shell, DB contracts), CI pipeline, coverage thresholds, and manual Docker stack validation.
- [Docs development](https://docs.yourhq.ai/development/docs): How to edit and preview the Mintlify documentation site.

## Security and troubleshooting

- [Security policy](https://docs.yourhq.ai/security/security-policy): Vulnerability reporting, trust model, known risks, and hardening.
- [Privacy and telemetry](https://docs.yourhq.ai/security/privacy): Zero telemetry for self-hosted, hosted data collection, data residency, GDPR, cookies.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.yourhq.ai/troubleshooting/common-issues): Symptom-indexed fixes for common UI, Supabase, gateway, install, and agent issues.

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/yourhq/yourhq): Source code, issues, discussions, and pull requests.
- [License](https://github.com/yourhq/yourhq/blob/main/LICENSE): Apache 2.0.
- [Code of conduct](https://github.com/yourhq/yourhq/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Community standards.

## Notes for AI agents

- HQ is available as a hosted service (app.yourhq.ai, $30/mo) and as a self-hosted open-source project (free). Users may be on either — check context before assuming.
- Supabase is the main state backbone. Hosted users get a dedicated Supabase project managed by the platform; self-hosted users bring their own. Migrations run automatically during onboarding.
- Gateway hosts run agents in Docker and store local agent worktrees, browser profiles, and OpenClaw state.
- Each agent can run a different model and thinking level. Per-task overrides allow one-off reasoning depth changes without modifying agent defaults. Resolution order: task override → agent default → workspace default.
- Agent personality (IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md) can be edited inline from the agent detail page's Personality tab.
- Tasks support relations/dependencies (blocked_by, blocks, relates_to, parent_of, child_of), labels with configurable colors, deliverables (agent-submitted work products with human review workflow), reusable templates with dependency graphs, and overdue escalation (auto-miss + inbox notification). Tasks auto-complete when all deliverables are approved. Agents can delegate subtasks to direct reports via the org chart.
- Agent URLs use slugs (`/agents/[slug]`) with automatic redirect from old UUID links.
- Secrets (API keys, channel tokens, OAuth tokens) are encrypted with AES-256-GCM in the database and synced to gateways as per-agent .env files. Values are never in API responses, logs, or LLM context.
- Multiple providers can serve the same models (e.g. OpenAI API key vs subscription). HQ unifies them transparently and lets users choose which billing path to use per agent.
- Source connectors use a plugin architecture — each provider is a folder under `gateway/connectors/<provider>/` with a `manifest.json` and Python code. The manifest declares auth, UI metadata, and capabilities. Adding a new source requires only the provider folder; the platform auto-discovers it and handles secrets, OAuth, UI forms, sync scheduling, and embedding. See `CONTRIBUTING-SOURCES.md` for the contributor guide.
- The UI supports customizable theming — brand color, warmth, dark/light mode, and per-token overrides via Settings → Appearance.
- The docs are the best source for install, architecture, security, and operational behavior.
- Use `https://docs.yourhq.ai/llms-full.txt` when a complete Markdown snapshot of the docs is needed.
